name: Sam Reynolds
bio: Sam Reynolds is a former professional soccer goalkeeper who played five seasons across MLS and USL — earning his MLS debut with Toronto FC and training under legendary U.S. national team coach Bob Bradley at Chivas USA. He was invited to a U.S. Men's National Team training camp, won the George F. Donnelly Cup MVP award, and played international exhibitions against Sunderland, Chivas Guadalajara, and the Haiti national team. After retiring, he spent eight years as a professional soccer agent before joining the University of San Francisco in 2019, where he's developed a WCC Goalkeeper of the Year and serves as the primary recruiting coordinator.

timeline.title: Davis Senior High School
timeline.detail: Played for coach Ashley Yudin's Blue Devils — undefeated for two seasons with two CIF Section championships. Graduated in 2000.

timeline.title: California State Cup Champion (U-18)
timeline.detail: Won the California State Cup Championship with the River City Rush (CYSL) under coach Marcos Mercado.

timeline.title: Cal State Fullerton — Big West Honorable Mention
timeline.detail: Four-time letter winner. 59 matches, 57 starts, 12 shutouts, 1.51 GAA under head coach Al Mistri. Red-shirted freshman year.

timeline.title: George F. Donnelly Cup — MVP
timeline.detail: Selected for the USASA California-South Region IV team. Won the national amateur championship. Named tournament MVP.

timeline.title: Portland Timbers — First Pro Contract
timeline.detail: Signed April 2, 2005. Played in a friendly against Sunderland of the English Premier League in July 2005.

timeline.title: MLS Pool → Chivas USA
timeline.detail: League Pool Goalkeeper under Bob Bradley and GK coach Zak Abdel. Exhibition matches vs. Necaxa in Aguascalientes, Mexico.

timeline.title: MLS Debut — Toronto FC ⭐
timeline.detail: July 1: MLS debut in a 1–1 draw vs. Kansas City Wizards at Arrowhead Stadium. July 4: Started again, earned 2–1 win over Real Salt Lake. Exhibition vs. Chivas Guadalajara at Estadio Jalisco, November.

timeline.title: Miami FC — Shutout King
timeline.detail: 3 starts, 0.33 GAA. Two shutout victories (Rochester Rhinos, Atlanta Silverbacks). Friendly vs. Haiti national team. Invited to U.S. Men's National Team training camp in September.

timeline.title: Austin Aztex — Final Season
timeline.detail: 12 appearances — his most in any single professional season. Released at end of year.

timeline.title: Cal State Fullerton — GK Coach
timeline.detail: Returned to alma mater. Coached goalkeepers for both men's and women's programs.

timeline.title: U.S. National Beach Soccer Team
timeline.detail: Played goalkeeper at the Cancún Cup under coach Eddie Soto.

timeline.title: Professional Soccer Agent — PROfc
timeline.detail: Eight years working as a Professional Football Consultant with PROfc (co-founded by former Timbers/CSU Fullerton teammate Shaun Higgins). Worked with recruits, scouts, GMs, owners, and sponsors. Negotiated contracts and placed players from MLS to top leagues worldwide.

timeline.title: USF Dons — Assistant Coach & GK Coach
timeline.detail: Named assistant by head coach Leonard Griffin. Primary recruiting coordinator. Developed Eric Waltz (WCC GK of the Year 2023), Alejandro Munoz, and Brandon Keniston. Holds UEFA B License.

videos.title: MLS Debut: Toronto FC @ Kansas City Wizards
videos.description: Sam Reynolds makes his MLS debut in goal for Toronto FC — a 1–1 draw at Arrowhead Stadium against the Kansas City Wizards.

videos.title: USF Dons vs Saint Mary's College Highlights
videos.description: USF Dons men's soccer highlights featuring the program Sam Reynolds helps lead as assistant coach and goalkeeper coach.

internationalExhibitions.context: Friendly — against the English Premier League side

internationalExhibitions.match: Miami FC vs Haiti National Team
U.S. Men's National Team Training Camp
internationalExhibitions.context: Invited — World Cup qualifier prep vs Cuba
Cancún Cup — under coach Eddie Soto

articles.title: Former Devil Reynolds continues in lofty soccer circles
Former Titan Stars in MLS Debuts
Samuel's PK vaults Toronto FC past Salt Lake 2-1
Throwback Thursday — Timbers vs Austin Aztex

quotes.text: Known for his leadership, work ethic and character on and off the field.
I brought in Javier Ayala and Sam Reynolds as assistant coaches because we share the same vision for the future of USF soccer.
quotes.source: Leonard Griffin, former USF Head Coach (2020)

quotes.text: As a junior, Sam was behind two very good goalies. He waited patiently for his turn.
quotes.source: Ashley Yudin, Davis HS Coach — Davis Enterprise

name: Carla Reynolds
currentRole: Managing Director, Client Service Field Operations & Development Executive
bio: Carla Reynolds is a Managing Director at Merrill Lynch (Bank of America) in San Francisco, leading Client Service Field Operations and Development. A former NCAA Division I tennis player at Cal State Fullerton, she brings the same competitive drive to the world of finance. She holds a Doctor of Education (Ed.D.) from Pepperdine University and has spent 16+ years building her career at one of the world's most prestigious financial institutions. Originally from Southern California, Carla now calls San Francisco home.

timeline.title: Cal State Fullerton — NCAA Division I Tennis
timeline.detail: Competed on the Titans women's tennis team in the Big West Conference. The same school where her future husband Sam Reynolds played soccer.

timeline.title: Pepperdine University — Ed.D.
timeline.detail: Earned a Doctor of Education degree from Pepperdine's Graduate School of Education and Psychology — one of the most prestigious education programs on the West Coast.

timeline.title: Joined Merrill Lynch
timeline.detail: Began her career at Merrill Lynch, Pierce, Fenner & Smith Inc. in San Francisco. Passed the Series 66 examination, becoming a registered securities agent and investment advisor.

timeline.title: Managing Director — Merrill Lynch
timeline.detail: Leads Client Service Field Operations and Development at Merrill Lynch (Bank of America). Over 16 years at the firm with a focus on service excellence, team development, and operational leadership.

careerHighlights.title: Managing Director
careerHighlights.detail: Senior leadership role at one of the world's largest wealth management firms

careerHighlights.title: Client Service Field Operations & Development
careerHighlights.detail: Leads operational excellence and professional development across the field organization

careerHighlights.title: Doctor of Education
careerHighlights.detail: Ed.D. from Pepperdine University — combining education expertise with financial leadership

careerHighlights.title: Series 66 Licensed
careerHighlights.detail: Registered securities agent and investment advisor in California

careerHighlights.title: NCAA Division I Athlete
careerHighlights.detail: Competed in women's tennis at Cal State Fullerton in the Big West Conference
